what_it_does: Defines the module constitution for the tests/ directory, prescribing how acceptance suites are structured, run, and used as executable proofs of capabilities.
observations: Declares itself a living constitution with a prescriptive register for the tests module.
Mandates one runnable acceptance suite per capability, named tests/<name>_acceptance.py.
Requires suites to be run via a shell loop over tests/*.py using .venv/bin/python.
States a capability is real only when backed by a green suite or fresh use-evidence.
Forbids testing against mocks when the real system can be exercised.
Directs the live suite index to [[Company State]] and warns against duplicating it here.
References related constitutions for runtime, nodes, store, and self-modification gates.
Emphasizes adding suites that fail before a capability exists and pass after it is built.
standouts: The 'prove by use' standard: operate the live system, not just assert.
The self-joining run-all loop keeps proof and execution in step.
Self-modification gates are guarded by loud-failing regression suites.
conventions: Follows a constitution template with front-matter type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Uses wiki-style internal links such as [[Company State]] for cross-references.
Avoids duplicating the suite list by delegating the live index to another file.
Uses an imperative structure: Is / Guarantees / Where new things go / To extend / Seam / Never.
concerns: The run command is shown inline but the file does not say where that loop is encoded or enforced.
The auto-maintained index and drift-check are referenced but their mechanics are not visible here.
The 'living' status is asserted without showing a revision or review mechanism in this file.
notes: This is the prescriptive constitution for the tests module: it treats acceptance suites as executable proofs and forbids mock-based testing when real interfaces are available.
questions: What exact mechanism auto-maintains the live suite index in [[Company State]]?
How is the drift-check that fails when a registered capability lacks a suite implemented?
Where is the shell loop 'for t in tests/*.py; do ./.venv/bin/python "$t"; done' stored or enforced?
What specific criteria distinguish 'fresh use-evidence' from a green suite?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a prescriptive constitution for the tests module that defines acceptance-suite rules.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the test suite the authoritative, self-updating proof record of what the system can actually do, while preventing drift into assertion-only or mock-heavy verification.
novelty: Treats each acceptance test file as a constitution-level proof that a capability exists, with a self-joining run loop and a 'prove by use' standard that elevates operational evidence above green tests alone.
contribution: Provides a prescriptive, module-level rule set that keeps acceptance tests aligned with real capabilities, mandates real-interface verification, and delegates the live suite index to another file to avoid duplication.
summary_for_embedding: The tests/ module constitution prescribing acceptance suites as runnable proofs of capabilities, one suite per capability, run via a shell loop, with a ban on mock testing and a 'prove by use' standard.
intention_for_embedding: To establish tests/ as the convergence record where every capability is proven by executing real interfaces rather than asserted or mocked.
